TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_ami_Jayanth_Reddy, title = Jayanth_Reddy, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Amis]}, url = {https://ami.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jayanth_Reddy} }

S.FORM: Jayanth Reddy is a Martial Arts and Tae Kwon Do expert from Hyderabad, India. = Early Life = Jayanth was born in Hyderabad on 14 December 1962 to Vittal Reddy and Shivamma. He did his schooling from Vijaya School and later joined Mani Jeremiah High School. He completed his Intermediate from Aliya Junior College. Jayanth was a very good cricketer and used to also play soccer. It all started in 1976 when his brothers Krishna Reddy and Sridhar Reddy brought him a book on martial arts, which led him into the classes of traditional Karate. After four years of rigorous practice, he earned a Black Belt in karate but that did not stop him from exploring other material art forms as his fascination to deliver those high kicks in mid-air paved the way for the beginning of an epic journey into the world of Taekwondo. At the age of 12, he was inspired by Bruce Lee's Enter the Dragon and one thing led to another. Initially, his parents were sceptical about it and thought he would end up as a gangster. But as he started to win awards in the martial arts circuit, their attitude changed completely. His brothers gave classes on traditional karate and he was coached by Narsimha and Srinivasan. The turning point in his career came when Manoj Tiwari motivated him to take up Taekwondo. Apart from being a coach and a contender, he also distinguished himself as an organiser by conducting a South Zone Championship in 1983. After staging numerous Taekwondo demonstrations across India and conducting several districts, state, zonal and National-level Championships, he started exploring this art in greater depth and that is what took him to the United States, where he underwent a six-month advanced training camp under the watchful eyes of two 9th Degree black belters Moo Yang Lee and Pong Ki Kim. He followed it up with an advanced course in speed training once again in the US. This enabled to earn the 4th to 6th Dan Black Belt titles from various world-renowned Martial Arts Associations. He also availed the unique opportunity of training with several Grand Masters and athletes of over 50 countries across the globe which helped him to stay updated by gaining several modern techniques. Jayanth's main goal is to help Indians create world records in Martial arts. A total of 2,000 people have undergone training at his academy ever since its inception in 2010. He is now coming up with a first of its kind Martial Arts league in India. "Martial Arts has given a lot to me and now I want to give the sport back in any way possible to increase its popularity in the next 10 years. I wish to help another 10,000 Indians achieve world records. This will improve India's reputation in the world of Martial Arts, which is currently dominated by countries like China, Japan, Korea and Thailand," he said. = Professional Career = « Achieved 29 Guinness World Records « Achieved 12 US Presidential Sports awards (1993, 1998, 2005, 2010 and 2020) from President of the USA « Achieved 12 world records from Limca, RHR, Asian etc. from 2012 to 2015 « Kukkiwon, Korea - honored appreciation award 2013, 2016 and 2019 « Awarded citation from World Taekwondo Federation, Korea « Awarded with Hall of Fame USA in 1999 « International Champion in 1999-2000 « National and National games Champion in 1983, 84, 85, 86 and 1987 « 8th DAN Black Belt from Taekwondo Jidokwan, Korea « 8th DAN Black Belt from World Taekwondo Masters Association = As an Author = A subset of books authored by Mr. Jayanth Reddy « Secrets of Longevity Revealed « The Hidden Secrets of Martial Arts « Secrets of advanced Explosive Speed Kicking and Speed punching « Dynamic power of mind « Secrets of Advanced Speed fighting « Humans destroying the god « The Most Inspirational and Motivational quotes of success in Martial arts, Sports, Mind , Life and God « Martial yoga « 9 minutes Fitness workout « The Ancient secret of preventive exercises for "CORONA VIRUS" « Secrets of 3 minutes workout « Secrets of 30 minutes workout « Advanced World Taekwondo Training = See Also = Jayanth on YouTube Jayanth on Google Scholar = External Links = Instagram LinkedIn
S.PHON: *ajantħ rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj is a martiaɾ arts anɬ~ɮ taə kwo~un ɬ~ɮo~u əxpərt fro~um ħjɬ~ɮərab~b~vaɬ~ɮ, inɬ~ɮia. = əarɾj ɾifə = *ajantħ was b~b~vo~urn in ħjɬ~ɮərab~b~vaɬ~ɮ o~un ** ɬ~ɮəʦəmb~b~vər **** to~u b~vittaɾ rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj anɬ~ɮ sħib~vamma. ħə ɬ~ɮiɬ~ɮ ħis sʦħo~uo~uɾiŋ fro~um b~vijaja sʦħo~uo~uɾ anɬ~ɮ ɾatər jo~uinəɬ~ɮ mani *ərəmiaħ ħi*ħ sʦħo~uo~uɾ. ħə ʦo~umpɾətəɬ~ɮ ħis intərməɬ~ɮiatə fro~um aɾija *o~u~unio~ur ʦo~uɾɾə*ə. *ajantħ was a b~vərj *o~uo~uɬ~ɮ ʦriʦkətər anɬ~ɮ o~u~usəɬ~ɮ to~u aɾso~u pɾaj so~uʦʦər. it aɾɾ startəɬ~ɮ in **** wħən ħis b~b~vro~utħərs krisħna rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj anɬ~ɮ sriɬ~ɮħar rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj b~b~vro~uo~u~u*ħt ħim a b~b~vo~uo~uk o~un martiaɾ arts, wħiʦħ ɾəɬ~ɮ ħim into~u tħə ʦɾassəs o~uf traɬ~ɮitio~unaɾ karatə. aftər fo~uo~u~ur jəars o~uf ri*o~uro~uo~u~us praʦtiʦə, ħə əarnəɬ~ɮ a b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt in karatə b~b~vo~u~ut tħat ɬ~ɮiɬ~ɮ no~ut sto~up ħim fro~um əxpɾo~uriŋ o~utħər matəriaɾ art fo~urms as ħis fasʦinatio~un to~u ɬ~ɮəɾib~vər tħo~usə ħi*ħ kiʦks in miɬ~ɮ-air pab~vəɬ~ɮ tħə waj fo~ur tħə b~b~və*inniŋ o~uf an əpiʦ jo~uo~u~urnəj into~u tħə wo~urɾɬ~ɮ o~uf taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u. at tħə a*ə o~uf **, ħə was inspirəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj b~b~vro~u~uʦə ɾəəʡs əntər tħə ɬ~ɮra*o~un anɬ~ɮ o~unə tħiŋ ɾəɬ~ɮ to~u ano~utħər. initiaɾɾj, ħis parənts wərə sʦəptiʦaɾ ab~b~vo~uo~u~ut it anɬ~ɮ tħo~uo~u~u*ħt ħə wo~uo~u~uɾɬ~ɮ ənɬ~ɮ o~u~up as a *aŋstər. b~b~vo~u~ut as ħə startəɬ~ɮ to~u win awarɬ~ɮs in tħə martiaɾ arts ʦirʦo~u~uit, tħəir attito~u~uɬ~ɮə ʦħaŋəɬ~ɮ ʦo~umpɾətəɾj. ħis b~b~vro~utħərs *ab~və ʦɾassəs o~un traɬ~ɮitio~unaɾ karatə anɬ~ɮ ħə was ʦo~uaʦħəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj narsimħa anɬ~ɮ srinib~vasan. tħə to~u~urniŋ po~uint in ħis ʦarəər ʦamə wħən mano~uj tiwari mo~utib~vatəɬ~ɮ ħim to~u takə o~u~up taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u. apart fro~um b~b~vəiŋ a ʦo~uaʦħ anɬ~ɮ a ʦo~untənɬ~ɮər, ħə aɾso~u ɬ~ɮistiŋo~u~uisħəɬ~ɮ ħimsəɾf as an o~ur*anisər b~b~vj ʦo~unɬ~ɮo~u~uʦtiŋ a so~uo~u~utħ *o~unə ʦħampio~unsħip in ****. aftər sta*iŋ no~u~uməro~uo~u~us taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u ɬ~ɮəmo~unstratio~uns aʦro~uss inɬ~ɮia anɬ~ɮ ʦo~unɬ~ɮo~u~uʦtiŋ səb~vəraɾ ɬ~ɮistriʦts, statə, *o~unaɾ anɬ~ɮ natio~unaɾ-ɾəb~vəɾ ʦħampio~unsħips, ħə startəɬ~ɮ əxpɾo~uriŋ tħis art in *rəatər ɬ~ɮəptħ anɬ~ɮ tħat is wħat to~uo~uk ħim to~u tħə o~u~unitəɬ~ɮ statəs, wħərə ħə o~u~unɬ~ɮərwənt a six-mo~untħ aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ trainiŋ ʦamp o~u~unɬ~ɮər tħə watʦħfo~u~uɾ əjəs o~uf two~u *tħ ɬ~ɮə*rəə b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾtərs mo~uo~u jaŋ ɾəə anɬ~ɮ po~uŋ ki kim. ħə fo~uɾɾo~uwəɬ~ɮ it o~u~up witħ an aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ ʦo~uo~u~ursə in spəəɬ~ɮ trainiŋ o~unʦə a*ain in tħə o~u~us. tħis ənab~b~vɾəɬ~ɮ to~u əarn tħə *tħ to~u *tħ ɬ~ɮan b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt titɾəs fro~um b~vario~uo~u~us wo~urɾɬ~ɮ-rəno~uwnəɬ~ɮ martiaɾ arts asso~uʦiatio~uns. ħə aɾso~u ab~vaiɾəɬ~ɮ tħə o~u~uni*o~u~uə o~uppo~urto~u~unitj o~uf trainiŋ witħ səb~vəraɾ *ranɬ~ɮ mastərs anɬ~ɮ atħɾətəs o~uf o~ub~vər ** ʦo~uo~u~untriəs aʦro~uss tħə *ɾo~ub~b~və wħiʦħ ħəɾpəɬ~ɮ ħim to~u staj o~u~upɬ~ɮatəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj *ainiŋ səb~vəraɾ mo~uɬ~ɮərn təʦħni*o~u~uəs. *ajantħʡs main *o~uaɾ is to~u ħəɾp inɬ~ɮians ʦrəatə wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs in martiaɾ arts. a to~utaɾ o~uf *,*** pəo~upɾə ħab~və o~u~unɬ~ɮər*o~unə trainiŋ at ħis aʦaɬ~ɮəmj əb~vər sinʦə its inʦəptio~un in ****. ħə is no~uw ʦo~umiŋ o~u~up witħ a first o~uf its kinɬ~ɮ martiaɾ arts ɾəa*o~u~uə in inɬ~ɮia. "martiaɾ arts ħas *ib~vən a ɾo~ut to~u mə anɬ~ɮ no~uw i want to~u *ib~və tħə spo~urt b~b~vaʦk in anj waj po~ussib~b~vɾə to~u inʦrəasə its po~upo~u~uɾaritj in tħə nəxt ** jəars. i wisħ to~u ħəɾp ano~utħər **,*** inɬ~ɮians aʦħiəb~və wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs. tħis wiɾɾ impro~ub~və inɬ~ɮiaʡs rəpo~u~utatio~un in tħə wo~urɾɬ~ɮ o~uf martiaɾ arts, wħiʦħ is ʦo~u~urrəntɾj ɬ~ɮo~uminatəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj ʦo~uo~u~untriəs ɾikə ʦħina, *apan, ko~urəa anɬ~ɮ tħaiɾanɬ~ɮ," ħə saiɬ~ɮ. = pro~ufəssio~unaɾ ʦarəər = * aʦħiəb~vəɬ~ɮ ** *o~u~uinnəss wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs * aʦħiəb~vəɬ~ɮ ** o~u~us prəsiɬ~ɮəntiaɾ spo~urts awarɬ~ɮs (****, ****, ****, **** anɬ~ɮ ****) fro~um prəsiɬ~ɮənt o~uf tħə o~u~usa * aʦħiəb~vəɬ~ɮ ** wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs fro~um ɾimʦa, rħr, asian ətʦ. fro~um **** to~u **** * ko~u~ukkiwo~un, ko~urəa - ħo~uno~urəɬ~ɮ apprəʦiatio~un awarɬ~ɮ ****, **** anɬ~ɮ **** * awarɬ~ɮəɬ~ɮ ʦitatio~un fro~um wo~urɾɬ~ɮ taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u fəɬ~ɮəratio~un, ko~urəa * awarɬ~ɮəɬ~ɮ witħ ħaɾɾ o~uf famə o~u~usa in **** * intərnatio~unaɾ ʦħampio~un in ****-**** * natio~unaɾ anɬ~ɮ natio~unaɾ *aməs ʦħampio~un in ****, **, **, ** anɬ~ɮ **** * *tħ ɬ~ɮan b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt fro~um taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u *iɬ~ɮo~ukwan, ko~urəa * *tħ ɬ~ɮan b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt fro~um wo~urɾɬ~ɮ taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u mastərs asso~uʦiatio~un = as an ao~u~utħo~ur = a so~u~ub~b~vsət o~uf b~b~vo~uo~uks ao~u~utħo~urəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj mr. *ajantħ rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj * səʦrəts o~uf ɾo~uŋəb~vitj rəb~vəaɾəɬ~ɮ * tħə ħiɬ~ɮɬ~ɮən səʦrəts o~uf martiaɾ arts * səʦrəts o~uf aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ əxpɾo~usib~və spəəɬ~ɮ kiʦkiŋ anɬ~ɮ spəəɬ~ɮ po~u~unʦħiŋ * ɬ~ɮjnamiʦ po~uwər o~uf minɬ~ɮ * səʦrəts o~uf aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ spəəɬ~ɮ fi*ħtiŋ * ħo~u~umans ɬ~ɮəstro~ujiŋ tħə *o~uɬ~ɮ * tħə mo~ust inspiratio~unaɾ anɬ~ɮ mo~utib~vatio~unaɾ *o~u~uo~utəs o~uf so~u~uʦʦəss in martiaɾ arts, spo~urts, minɬ~ɮ , ɾifə anɬ~ɮ *o~uɬ~ɮ * martiaɾ jo~u*a * * mino~u~utəs fitnəss wo~urko~uo~u~ut * tħə anʦiənt səʦrət o~uf prəb~vəntib~və əxərʦisəs fo~ur "ʦo~uro~una b~viro~u~us" * səʦrəts o~uf * mino~u~utəs wo~urko~uo~u~ut * səʦrəts o~uf ** mino~u~utəs wo~urko~uo~u~ut * aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ wo~urɾɬ~ɮ taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u trainiŋ = səə aɾso~u = *ajantħ o~un jo~uo~u~uto~u~ub~b~və *ajantħ o~un *o~uo~u*ɾə sʦħo~uɾar = əxtərnaɾ ɾinks = insta*ram ɾinkəɬ~ɮin
S.FORM: Jayanth Reddy is a Martial Arts and Tae Kwon Do expert from Hyderabad, India. = Early Life = Jayanth was born in Hyderabad on 14 December 1962 to Vittal Reddy and Shivamma. He did his schooling from Vijaya School and later joined Mani Jeremiah High School. He completed his Intermediate from Aliya Junior College. Jayanth was a very good cricketer and used to also play soccer. It all started in 1976 when his brothers Krishna Reddy and Sridhar Reddy brought him a book on martial arts, which led him into the classes of traditional Karate. After four years of rigorous practice, he earned a Black Belt in karate but that did not stop him from exploring other material art forms as his fascination to deliver those high kicks in mid-air paved the way for the beginning of an epic journey into the world of Taekwondo. At the age of 12, he was inspired by Bruce Lee's Enter the Dragon and one thing led to another. Initially, his parents were sceptical about it and thought he would end up as a gangster. But as he started to win awards in the martial arts circuit, their attitude changed completely. His brothers gave classes on traditional karate and he was coached by Narsimha and Srinivasan. The turning point in his career came when Manoj Tiwari motivated him to take up Taekwondo. Apart from being a coach and a contender, he also distinguished himself as an organiser by conducting a South Zone Championship in 1983. After staging numerous Taekwondo demonstrations across India and conducting several districts, state, zonal and National-level Championships, he started exploring this art in greater depth and that is what took him to the United States, where he underwent a six-month advanced training camp under the watchful eyes of two 9th Degree black belters Moo Yang Lee and Pong Ki Kim. He followed it up with an advanced course in speed training once again in the US. This enabled to earn the 4th to 6th Dan Black Belt titles from various world-renowned Martial Arts Associations. He also availed the unique opportunity of training with several Grand Masters and athletes of over 50 countries across the globe which helped him to stay updated by gaining several modern techniques. Jayanth's main goal is to help Indians create world records in Martial arts. A total of 2,000 people have undergone training at his academy ever since its inception in 2010. He is now coming up with a first of its kind Martial Arts league in India. "Martial Arts has given a lot to me and now I want to give the sport back in any way possible to increase its popularity in the next 10 years. I wish to help another 10,000 Indians achieve world records. This will improve India's reputation in the world of Martial Arts, which is currently dominated by countries like China, Japan, Korea and Thailand," he said. = Professional Career = « Achieved 29 Guinness World Records « Achieved 12 US Presidential Sports awards (1993, 1998, 2005, 2010 and 2020) from President of the USA « Achieved 12 world records from Limca, RHR, Asian etc. from 2012 to 2015 « Kukkiwon, Korea - honored appreciation award 2013, 2016 and 2019 « Awarded citation from World Taekwondo Federation, Korea « Awarded with Hall of Fame USA in 1999 « International Champion in 1999-2000 « National and National games Champion in 1983, 84, 85, 86 and 1987 « 8th DAN Black Belt from Taekwondo Jidokwan, Korea « 8th DAN Black Belt from World Taekwondo Masters Association = As an Author = A subset of books authored by Mr. Jayanth Reddy « Secrets of Longevity Revealed « The Hidden Secrets of Martial Arts « Secrets of advanced Explosive Speed Kicking and Speed punching « Dynamic power of mind « Secrets of Advanced Speed fighting « Humans destroying the god « The Most Inspirational and Motivational quotes of success in Martial arts, Sports, Mind , Life and God « Martial yoga « 9 minutes Fitness workout « The Ancient secret of preventive exercises for "CORONA VIRUS" « Secrets of 3 minutes workout « Secrets of 30 minutes workout « Advanced World Taekwondo Training = See Also = Jayanth on YouTube Jayanth on Google Scholar = External Links = Instagram LinkedIn
S.PHON: *ajantħ rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj is a martiaɾ arts anɬ~ɮ taə kwo~un ɬ~ɮo~u əxpərt fro~um ħjɬ~ɮərab~b~vaɬ~ɮ, inɬ~ɮia. = əarɾj ɾifə = *ajantħ was b~b~vo~urn in ħjɬ~ɮərab~b~vaɬ~ɮ o~un ** ɬ~ɮəʦəmb~b~vər **** to~u b~vittaɾ rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj anɬ~ɮ sħib~vamma. ħə ɬ~ɮiɬ~ɮ ħis sʦħo~uo~uɾiŋ fro~um b~vijaja sʦħo~uo~uɾ anɬ~ɮ ɾatər jo~uinəɬ~ɮ mani *ərəmiaħ ħi*ħ sʦħo~uo~uɾ. ħə ʦo~umpɾətəɬ~ɮ ħis intərməɬ~ɮiatə fro~um aɾija *o~u~unio~ur ʦo~uɾɾə*ə. *ajantħ was a b~vərj *o~uo~uɬ~ɮ ʦriʦkətər anɬ~ɮ o~u~usəɬ~ɮ to~u aɾso~u pɾaj so~uʦʦər. it aɾɾ startəɬ~ɮ in **** wħən ħis b~b~vro~utħərs krisħna rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj anɬ~ɮ sriɬ~ɮħar rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj b~b~vro~uo~u~u*ħt ħim a b~b~vo~uo~uk o~un martiaɾ arts, wħiʦħ ɾəɬ~ɮ ħim into~u tħə ʦɾassəs o~uf traɬ~ɮitio~unaɾ karatə. aftər fo~uo~u~ur jəars o~uf ri*o~uro~uo~u~us praʦtiʦə, ħə əarnəɬ~ɮ a b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt in karatə b~b~vo~u~ut tħat ɬ~ɮiɬ~ɮ no~ut sto~up ħim fro~um əxpɾo~uriŋ o~utħər matəriaɾ art fo~urms as ħis fasʦinatio~un to~u ɬ~ɮəɾib~vər tħo~usə ħi*ħ kiʦks in miɬ~ɮ-air pab~vəɬ~ɮ tħə waj fo~ur tħə b~b~və*inniŋ o~uf an əpiʦ jo~uo~u~urnəj into~u tħə wo~urɾɬ~ɮ o~uf taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u. at tħə a*ə o~uf **, ħə was inspirəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj b~b~vro~u~uʦə ɾəəʡs əntər tħə ɬ~ɮra*o~un anɬ~ɮ o~unə tħiŋ ɾəɬ~ɮ to~u ano~utħər. initiaɾɾj, ħis parənts wərə sʦəptiʦaɾ ab~b~vo~uo~u~ut it anɬ~ɮ tħo~uo~u~u*ħt ħə wo~uo~u~uɾɬ~ɮ ənɬ~ɮ o~u~up as a *aŋstər. b~b~vo~u~ut as ħə startəɬ~ɮ to~u win awarɬ~ɮs in tħə martiaɾ arts ʦirʦo~u~uit, tħəir attito~u~uɬ~ɮə ʦħaŋəɬ~ɮ ʦo~umpɾətəɾj. ħis b~b~vro~utħərs *ab~və ʦɾassəs o~un traɬ~ɮitio~unaɾ karatə anɬ~ɮ ħə was ʦo~uaʦħəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj narsimħa anɬ~ɮ srinib~vasan. tħə to~u~urniŋ po~uint in ħis ʦarəər ʦamə wħən mano~uj tiwari mo~utib~vatəɬ~ɮ ħim to~u takə o~u~up taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u. apart fro~um b~b~vəiŋ a ʦo~uaʦħ anɬ~ɮ a ʦo~untənɬ~ɮər, ħə aɾso~u ɬ~ɮistiŋo~u~uisħəɬ~ɮ ħimsəɾf as an o~ur*anisər b~b~vj ʦo~unɬ~ɮo~u~uʦtiŋ a so~uo~u~utħ *o~unə ʦħampio~unsħip in ****. aftər sta*iŋ no~u~uməro~uo~u~us taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u ɬ~ɮəmo~unstratio~uns aʦro~uss inɬ~ɮia anɬ~ɮ ʦo~unɬ~ɮo~u~uʦtiŋ səb~vəraɾ ɬ~ɮistriʦts, statə, *o~unaɾ anɬ~ɮ natio~unaɾ-ɾəb~vəɾ ʦħampio~unsħips, ħə startəɬ~ɮ əxpɾo~uriŋ tħis art in *rəatər ɬ~ɮəptħ anɬ~ɮ tħat is wħat to~uo~uk ħim to~u tħə o~u~unitəɬ~ɮ statəs, wħərə ħə o~u~unɬ~ɮərwənt a six-mo~untħ aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ trainiŋ ʦamp o~u~unɬ~ɮər tħə watʦħfo~u~uɾ əjəs o~uf two~u *tħ ɬ~ɮə*rəə b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾtərs mo~uo~u jaŋ ɾəə anɬ~ɮ po~uŋ ki kim. ħə fo~uɾɾo~uwəɬ~ɮ it o~u~up witħ an aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ ʦo~uo~u~ursə in spəəɬ~ɮ trainiŋ o~unʦə a*ain in tħə o~u~us. tħis ənab~b~vɾəɬ~ɮ to~u əarn tħə *tħ to~u *tħ ɬ~ɮan b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt titɾəs fro~um b~vario~uo~u~us wo~urɾɬ~ɮ-rəno~uwnəɬ~ɮ martiaɾ arts asso~uʦiatio~uns. ħə aɾso~u ab~vaiɾəɬ~ɮ tħə o~u~uni*o~u~uə o~uppo~urto~u~unitj o~uf trainiŋ witħ səb~vəraɾ *ranɬ~ɮ mastərs anɬ~ɮ atħɾətəs o~uf o~ub~vər ** ʦo~uo~u~untriəs aʦro~uss tħə *ɾo~ub~b~və wħiʦħ ħəɾpəɬ~ɮ ħim to~u staj o~u~upɬ~ɮatəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj *ainiŋ səb~vəraɾ mo~uɬ~ɮərn təʦħni*o~u~uəs. *ajantħʡs main *o~uaɾ is to~u ħəɾp inɬ~ɮians ʦrəatə wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs in martiaɾ arts. a to~utaɾ o~uf *,*** pəo~upɾə ħab~və o~u~unɬ~ɮər*o~unə trainiŋ at ħis aʦaɬ~ɮəmj əb~vər sinʦə its inʦəptio~un in ****. ħə is no~uw ʦo~umiŋ o~u~up witħ a first o~uf its kinɬ~ɮ martiaɾ arts ɾəa*o~u~uə in inɬ~ɮia. "martiaɾ arts ħas *ib~vən a ɾo~ut to~u mə anɬ~ɮ no~uw i want to~u *ib~və tħə spo~urt b~b~vaʦk in anj waj po~ussib~b~vɾə to~u inʦrəasə its po~upo~u~uɾaritj in tħə nəxt ** jəars. i wisħ to~u ħəɾp ano~utħər **,*** inɬ~ɮians aʦħiəb~və wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs. tħis wiɾɾ impro~ub~və inɬ~ɮiaʡs rəpo~u~utatio~un in tħə wo~urɾɬ~ɮ o~uf martiaɾ arts, wħiʦħ is ʦo~u~urrəntɾj ɬ~ɮo~uminatəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj ʦo~uo~u~untriəs ɾikə ʦħina, *apan, ko~urəa anɬ~ɮ tħaiɾanɬ~ɮ," ħə saiɬ~ɮ. = pro~ufəssio~unaɾ ʦarəər = * aʦħiəb~vəɬ~ɮ ** *o~u~uinnəss wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs * aʦħiəb~vəɬ~ɮ ** o~u~us prəsiɬ~ɮəntiaɾ spo~urts awarɬ~ɮs (****, ****, ****, **** anɬ~ɮ ****) fro~um prəsiɬ~ɮənt o~uf tħə o~u~usa * aʦħiəb~vəɬ~ɮ ** wo~urɾɬ~ɮ rəʦo~urɬ~ɮs fro~um ɾimʦa, rħr, asian ətʦ. fro~um **** to~u **** * ko~u~ukkiwo~un, ko~urəa - ħo~uno~urəɬ~ɮ apprəʦiatio~un awarɬ~ɮ ****, **** anɬ~ɮ **** * awarɬ~ɮəɬ~ɮ ʦitatio~un fro~um wo~urɾɬ~ɮ taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u fəɬ~ɮəratio~un, ko~urəa * awarɬ~ɮəɬ~ɮ witħ ħaɾɾ o~uf famə o~u~usa in **** * intərnatio~unaɾ ʦħampio~un in ****-**** * natio~unaɾ anɬ~ɮ natio~unaɾ *aməs ʦħampio~un in ****, **, **, ** anɬ~ɮ **** * *tħ ɬ~ɮan b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt fro~um taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u *iɬ~ɮo~ukwan, ko~urəa * *tħ ɬ~ɮan b~b~vɾaʦk b~b~vəɾt fro~um wo~urɾɬ~ɮ taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u mastərs asso~uʦiatio~un = as an ao~u~utħo~ur = a so~u~ub~b~vsət o~uf b~b~vo~uo~uks ao~u~utħo~urəɬ~ɮ b~b~vj mr. *ajantħ rəɬ~ɮɬ~ɮj * səʦrəts o~uf ɾo~uŋəb~vitj rəb~vəaɾəɬ~ɮ * tħə ħiɬ~ɮɬ~ɮən səʦrəts o~uf martiaɾ arts * səʦrəts o~uf aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ əxpɾo~usib~və spəəɬ~ɮ kiʦkiŋ anɬ~ɮ spəəɬ~ɮ po~u~unʦħiŋ * ɬ~ɮjnamiʦ po~uwər o~uf minɬ~ɮ * səʦrəts o~uf aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ spəəɬ~ɮ fi*ħtiŋ * ħo~u~umans ɬ~ɮəstro~ujiŋ tħə *o~uɬ~ɮ * tħə mo~ust inspiratio~unaɾ anɬ~ɮ mo~utib~vatio~unaɾ *o~u~uo~utəs o~uf so~u~uʦʦəss in martiaɾ arts, spo~urts, minɬ~ɮ , ɾifə anɬ~ɮ *o~uɬ~ɮ * martiaɾ jo~u*a * * mino~u~utəs fitnəss wo~urko~uo~u~ut * tħə anʦiənt səʦrət o~uf prəb~vəntib~və əxərʦisəs fo~ur "ʦo~uro~una b~viro~u~us" * səʦrəts o~uf * mino~u~utəs wo~urko~uo~u~ut * səʦrəts o~uf ** mino~u~utəs wo~urko~uo~u~ut * aɬ~ɮb~vanʦəɬ~ɮ wo~urɾɬ~ɮ taəkwo~unɬ~ɮo~u trainiŋ = səə aɾso~u = *ajantħ o~un jo~uo~u~uto~u~ub~b~və *ajantħ o~un *o~uo~u*ɾə sʦħo~uɾar = əxtərnaɾ ɾinks = insta*ram ɾinkəɬ~ɮin
